title: Helpdesk API (.NET)
shortDescription: Helpdesk ticket system built with Clean Architecture, EF Core, and PostgreSQL.
description: Full-featured helpdesk domain built in C# .NET 8. Implements a ticket state machine (Open → In Progress → Resolved / Cancelled) with role-based access control, SLA deadline tracking, domain events, and structured logging. Refresh token rotation with family invalidation. Auth audit events.

architecture.summary: Clean Architecture with four layers: Domain, Application, Infrastructure, and API. Domain layer has zero external dependencies. State transitions enforced at domain layer only. Use Cases injected directly via DI — no command bus.
architecture.diagram:
graph TD
  API[API Layer] --> APP[Application / Use Cases]
  APP --> DOM[Domain]
  APP --> INF[Infrastructure]
  INF --> DOM
  INF --> DB[(PostgreSQL)]

title: Helpdesk API (NestJS)
shortDescription: Same helpdesk domain reimplemented in TypeScript using Fastify, Prisma, and BullMQ.
description: TypeScript implementation of the same helpdesk domain. Uses Fastify for performance, Prisma as ORM, BullMQ for async event processing, and Vitest for testing. Demonstrates that domain invariants survive a complete stack change.

architecture.summary: Modular NestJS structure with dedicated modules per domain area. Domain logic isolated from the framework. BullMQ handles async event dispatch after persistence.
architecture.diagram:
graph TD
  API[Fastify / NestJS] --> MOD[Domain Modules]
  MOD --> DOM[Domain Layer]
  MOD --> PRI[Prisma ORM]
  PRI --> DB[(PostgreSQL)]
  MOD --> BULL[BullMQ]
  BULL --> WORKER[Event Workers]

title: Helpdesk API (Java)
shortDescription: Java implementation with Spring Boot, JPA, and JUnit 5 test coverage.
description: Java implementation of the helpdesk domain using Spring Boot 3 and Spring Data JPA. Applies the same architectural decisions — Clean Architecture, spec-first domain invariants, role-based authorization — in a strongly-typed enterprise stack.

architecture.summary: Layered architecture following Spring Boot conventions. Repository pattern via Spring Data JPA. Domain invariants enforced in the service layer. JUnit 5 for all business rule coverage.
architecture.diagram:
graph TD
  CTL[Controllers] --> SVC[Service Layer]
  SVC --> DOM[Domain]
  SVC --> REP[Repository]
  REP --> DB[(PostgreSQL)]
